question: In the days when the judges ruled, there was a _________ in the land (Ruth 1:1)
Elimelech and his wife, Naomi, moved to which country (Ruth 1:1)?
Elimelech and Naomi were originally from what town (Ruth 1:1)?
What were the names of Naomi’s two sons (Ruth 1:2)?
Who from Naomi’s family died (pick the best answer) (Ruth 1:3f)?
options: Both her sons and her husband

question: What were the names of the two Moabite women Naomi’s sons married (Ruth 1:3)?
Why did Naomi want to return to Bethlehem (Ruth 1:6)?
options: The Lord appeared to her in a dream and told her to
She had no place among the Moabites after her husband died
She heard the Lord was providing food there

question: What did Naomi ask her daughters-in-law to do (Ruth 1:8)?
options: To remember her in prayer as she returned
To go back to their mother’s homes in Moab
To eat with her before she leaves
To come back home with her to Judah

question: What does Ruth decide to do when Naomi leaves (Ruth 1:17)?
options: Go back to her mother’s home
Find a new husband in Moab

question: What does Orpah decide to do when Naomi leaves (Ruth 1:14)?
options: Go back to her people and her gods
Find a new husband in Moab

question: Why does Naomi urge her daughter-in-laws to return home (Ruth 1:13)?
options: She can’t provide for them as she has no land back in Bethelehem
They shouldn’t wait for her to have sons for them to marry
She can’t find another husband to support them
Her people would not accept Moabite women

question: What does Naomi claim the Lord has done to her (Ruth 1:13)?
options: Forgotten her in days without number
Brought her great pain and sorrow

question: Ruth tells Naomi that she will do all of the following EXCEPT (Ruth 1:16f)
options: Naomi’s God will be her God

question: What did Ruth tell Naomi not to do (Ruth 1:16)?
options: Tell her there was no hope
Wait until Naomi’s current sons would grow up

question: What does Naomi ask to be called when she returned to Bethlehem (Ruth 1:20)?
What does “Mara” mean to Naomi and the others (Ruth 1:20)?
Who does Naomi say has made her life bitter and afflicted her (Ruth 1:20)?
Where is Ruth originally from (Ruth 1:22)?
What was Naomi’s relationship to Boaz (Ruth 2:1)?
options: He was a relative of her husband
He was her husband’s best friend

question: What did Ruth tell Naomi that she could do in the fields (Ruth 2:2)?
How does the foreman respond to Boaz (Ruth 2:7)?
options: She asked to gather sheaves behind the harvesters
I didn’t see her until now
Naomi said the woman could work here until evening
She paid me to gather with the other harvesters

question: How did the foreman identify Ruth to Boaz (Ruth 2:6)
options: As the former wife of Mahlon
As an unknown woman who worked hard all day
As a Moabitess who came back from Moab
As a foreigner seeking grain from the field

question: What did Boaz tell Ruth that he had instructed his laborers not to do (Ruth 2:9)?
What does Boaz ask the Lord to do for Ruth?
options: To give her a husband in Israel
To repay her for her actions
To give her a long life

question: Where does Boaz say Ruth had taken refuge (Ruth 2:12)
options: Under the God of Israel’s wings
In the land of the Lord of Israel
In the home of her husband
Under the care of the shepherd of Israel

question: What does Boaz hope Ruth may continue to find (Ruth 2:13)?
options: Strength and courage in the God of Israel
A home in arms of the God of Israel
Favor in the eyes of the Lord
Food to care for her mother-in-law

question: What does Boaz offer Ruth at mealtime (Ruth 2:14)?
After dinner with Boaz and his servants what did Ruth get up to do (Ruth 2:15)?
options: Pray to the God of Israel

question: How much barley did Ruth gather from Boaz’s field (Ruth 2:17)
What did Ruth do with her gathered grain?
options: She sold it for two shekels of silver
She gave it to her mother-in-law
She gathered it in her basket at the field

question: Why does Naomi say it was good for Ruth to work for Boaz (Ruth 2:20)?
options: She will find a lot of grain in Boaz’s fields
The harvesters of Boaz were known to be kind

question: What two harvests did Ruth stay in Boaz’s fields for (Ruth 2:23)
Where is Boaz the evening Naomi tells Ruth to go to him (Ruth 3:2)?
Naomi tells Ruth to do all of the following before going to Boaz EXCEPT (Ruth 3:3)
options: To put on her best clothes

question: When Boaz had finished eating where did he lie down (Ruth 3:7)?
options: Just outside the gate of the city
In the middle of the threshing floor
In a tent his servants had set up
At the end of the grain pile

question: What did Ruth do when she went to Boaz at the threshing floor (Ruth 3:7)?
options: Uncovered his feet and laid down
Bowed down before him three times
Covered herself so he couldn’t see her

question: When Boaz awoke at the threshing floor what did he discover (Ruth 3:8)?
options: The figure of a woman in the darkness
What he feared was someone trying to rob his grain
A woman lying at his feet

question: What did Boaz ask Ruth when he discovered her at his feet (Ruth 3:9)?
What does Ruth ask of Boaz at his feet (Ruth 3:9)?
options: To let her live under his wings
To spread his garment over her

question: What does Ruth identify Boaz as at the threshing floor (Ruth 3:9)?
What does Boaz praise Ruth for at his feet (Ruth 3:10)?
options: For not going after younger men

question: What does Boaz tell Ruth he will do for her (Ruth 3:11)?
options: Provide food and shelter for her
Let her live in his house among his servant girls

question: Boaz mentions that all the townsmen know what about Ruth (Ruth 3:11)?
options: She is a beautiful and intelligent woman
She is a woman of noble character
She is loyal to her mother-in-law
She is a hard worker with a generous heart

question: If the kinsman-redeemer doesn’t do his duty, what will Boaz do?
options: He will let Ruth continue to work at his field
He will help her return to Moab and her family

question: What does Boaz tell Ruth before she leaves the floor (Ruth 3:14)?
options: To come back the next night
To go to the other guardian-redeemer
To not let anyone a woman was there

question: What did Boaz give to Ruth before she left him (Ruth 3:15)?
What did Naomi ask when Ruth returned from Boaz (Ruth 3:16)?
options: What is he going to do for you?
Did he listen to your words?

question: Why does Ruth say Boaz gave her the barley (Ruth 3:17)?
options: To provide food until the deal was arranged
To not go back to Naomi empty-handed
So she would remember his kindness

question: Naomi told Ruth Boaz would not be able to sleep until _______ (Ruth 3:18)
options: He met with the elders in the city gate
He settled the matter with the other kinsman-redeemer
He had secured Ruth as his wife

question: What does Boaz first tell the kinsmen-redeemer that Naomi is doing?
options: Looking for a manager for her field

question: What does Boaz say comes with Naomi’s land (Ruth 4:5)?
Why doesn’t the guardian redeemer take the land?
options: He wants Boaz to have the land

question: Earlier in Israel, how was a transfer of property finalized (Ruth 4:7)?
options: Giving one’s sandal to the other
Giving a goat to the other

question: What did the kinsmen-redeemer finally tell Boaz (Ruth 4:8)?
options: To buy the land for himself
He was not actually first in line

question: The women of Israel say Ruth is better to Naomi than ______ sons (Ruth 4:15)
Who takes care of Ruth’s son (Ruth 4:16)?
Who is the father of David (Ruth 4:22)?
Who is the father of Obed (Ruth 4:21)
How many names are listed in the genealogy at the end of Ruth (Ruth 4)?
Who is the final name in the genealogy at the end of Ruth (Ruth 4:22)?
